tweets.text:
Friendly reminder that because of the Permian boom, linefill increased US commercial crude storage figures by 135-150 million bbls. 

Excluding linefill of 135 million bbls, US commercial crude storage is already below the 2010-2014 range. 

418.2-135=283.2 

And we are still going lower for US commercial crude storage with operational minimums by the end of July.

tweets.text:
EIA reported -17.2 million bbls total crude. 

Commercial crude draw was -8.3 million bbls. 

Higher than expected crude draw was the result of 1) lower than expected US crude oil production + adjustment and 2) higher refinery throughput. 

No supply side surprise from US shale.

tweets.text:
Star at this point it’s clear Hyperliquid’s success comes at the cost of Binance’s market share. This is why CZ is so scared, a significant amount of Binance’s volume comes from white labeling deals with local exchanges engaged in jurisdictional arbitrage.

Given so much of OKX’s revenues are wallet based at this point it’s only logical any Hyperliquid market share gain hurts Binance.

You know what you need to do. Rip out OKX’s own equity trading perps and replace them with Hyperliquid’s.

Hyperliquid is the last chance we have at materializing the Crypto vision we were promised of open, standardized and liquid financial markets.

tweets.text:
The fact so many people have been brainwashed to believe the success of Bitcoin is dependent on one man and one company is what's beyond stupid.

I believe Strategy will continue to prioritize paying STRC dividends. They don't really have much of a choice.

The issue is - they're forced to either sell equity (aka MSTR ATM) or sell Bitcoin to fund those payments + debt obligations in the next couple of years.

With the recent Bitcoin purchases + cash raises, they've signaled their preferred route is diluting MSTR shareholders.

And unfortunately there most likely will be a tethered selloff at some point, but that doesn't mean either Strategy or Bitcoin will die.

Strategy will survive, but their cash raising vehicle will be impaired as they deal with rebuilding trust.

Bitcoin will bounce back and print new highs like it always has.

So yes, there easily can be a scenario where MSTR is significantly outperformed by BTC.

thread_tweets.text:
alright - let's go through their obligations in a WORST CASE SCENARIO

(well, not full worst case because Saylor being removed from his leadership position within the company is actually what would open the doors for pure chaos)

they have $6.7B of debt obligations they need CASH for in the next two years... that's a lot of $ (fyi - these convertibles used to basically always convert to common shares working like call options up until recently as the MSTR premium has collapsed)

they also have $1.7B/yr of dividends to payout to prefs like STRC.. this number will climb as they hike the yield to try and restore confidence in the $100 peg target

they keep shifting the metrics to justify selling MSTR as being accretive... but let's just go with the most recent one where Saylor said as long as the *adjusted* mNAV is >1 any sales are accretive

but say the MSTR premium continues to compress down to 1 or lower (which it should considering the continuing dilution)... e.g. that would look like MSTR dropping to $96 while Bitcoin maintains $65K, the ATM selling is no longer a viable or defensible option.

and they won't be able to raise any more $ via STRC as long as it's well off the $100 peg

so that's the scenario where they're forced to sell Bitcoin

and the market realizes this, gets spooked and tries to front run them so we probably do see a selloff before they actually sell a significant portion

realistically at the end of the day worst scenario they'd only need to sell ~$7B - $10B worth of Bitcoin to get out of this mess

the real question becomes *where* do they sell it... e.g. the sooner they pull the trigger and the higher they do it, the more of their balance sheet they preserve. I think they're gambling by waiting and just hoping number goes up so they can just defer these concerns for another day, and thus will be forced to sell much lower which will be more dilutive on a BPS term for MSTR holders

but when they finally resolve all of that their balance sheet will be fine, they'll be able to continue holding the rest of their stack and be able to survive

MSTR will just be extremely diluted and STRC has a good chance of just trading like junk bonds at that point though

tweets.text:
e.g. one man and one company don't define the success of BTC

if MSTR goes down significantly, could BTC sell off for a bit as a result?

sure

but it'll bounce back to new highs like it always has after every time it's been declared dead

meanwhile in that scenario, Strategy doesn't have to sell all of their Bitcoin - but MSTR gets diluted significantly and takes a good chunk of time to regain trust & have the ability to buy more accretively rather than being purely defensive

tweets.text:
The hardest thing in business is not seeing the future. It is surviving long enough to build it.

My fireside chat with @Julian_Liniger at @BTCPrague on focus, endurance, corporate transformation, and how entrepreneurs can use Bitcoin, AI, and digital finance to create the next generation of products.

Full interview below.

00:00 - Bitcoin as the dominant global Digital Capital network: 17 years, hundreds of billions invested, and a potential $100T opportunity
00:51 - Bitcoin near the 200-week moving average: why $BTC is more compelling after a 50% drawdown
01:52 - Strategy’s scale and the media narrative: from ~$600M enterprise value to as high as ~$120B
10:29 - Bitcoin fundamentals: economic empowerment, sovereign property rights, and the dominant digital monetary network
12:16 - Why there is no second best: Bitcoin as Digital Capital, Digital Money, and a potential $100T network
16:09 - Entrepreneur advice: build a simple product using new technology to solve a real problem
20:30 - Focus, endurance, and the danger of dilutive distractions
32:25 - What I would build today: AI plus Digital Assets, especially Digital Money and Digital Yield
33:27 - Digital Credit: taking a 40 vol asset, stripping it to ~4 vol, and creating new yield products
34:57 - Digital Money: 6–8% yield in major currencies with no volatility
38:05 - $STRC, $SATA, and the next layer of bitcoin-backed financial products
48:52 - Q&A: why Strategy sold 32 BTC and why bitcoin-backed capital must support credit and equity
59:29 - Q&A: Strategy as a shock absorber: selling 32 BTC while buying net ~250,000 BTC during the bear market
01:02:39 - Why public companies protect Bitcoin through accounting, tax, legal, political, and economic advocacy
01:07:58 - Strategy as the extension of the Bitcoin network into the free market system
